title: SUPPORT2 Audited Survival Benchmark: Principled Audit, Corrected Baselines, LODGO Transportability, and Open Reference Pipeline
description: Reproducible pipeline and full codebase accompanying the manuscript "Hidden Leakage in SUPPORT2 Survival Benchmarks: A Principled Audit, Corrected Baselines, and Open Reference Pipeline" (PLOS ONE, 2026). This release (v1.0) provides (i) a three-layer leakage audit of all 47 SUPPORT2 variables, (ii) bootstrap-validated baselines for four survival models on the audited feature set (GBM C-index = 0.7052, 95% CI 0.6861–0.7230), (iii) a leave-one-disease-group-out (LODGO) cross-validation benchmark across all eight diagnostic groups with bootstrap confidence intervals (mean C = 0.637, mean ΔC = −0.068 vs primary), (iv) a complete resumable pipeline with stage-based checkpointing and per-run manifest files, and (v) quickstart notebooks and smoke tests for downstream adoption. The SUPPORT2 dataset (9,105 ICU/hospital patients from Knaus 1995) is bundled to enable one-command reproduction of every figure and table in the paper.
references: Knaus WA, Harrell FE Jr, Lynn J, et al. The SUPPORT prognostic model: Objective estimates of survival for seriously ill hospitalized adults. Ann Intern Med. 1995;122(3):191-203.
Harrell FE Jr. Regression Modeling Strategies. 2nd ed. Springer; 2015.
Pölsterl S. scikit-survival: A Library for Time-to-Event Analysis Built on Top of scikit-learn. JMLR 2020;21(212):1-6.
